name: Zeebe
what_it_does: Camunda's cloud-native BPMN 2.0 workflow engine with a gRPC-based protocol that provides horizontally scalable, high-throughput process orchestration for microservices and AI agent workflows.
use_cases: Orchestrating AI agent steps as BPMN service tasks with visual process diagrams that stakeholders can review and understand
Running high-throughput event-driven workflows where millions of process instances must execute concurrently
Implementing message correlation patterns where agent steps publish events that trigger or resume waiting process instances
Coordinating human task assignment and approval steps within otherwise automated agent pipelines
Building compliance-auditable workflows where BPMN diagram and execution history satisfy regulatory requirements
not_for: Teams unfamiliar with BPMN notation who want code-first workflow definitions without visual diagram tooling
Simple sequential task chains that don't need the overhead of a full BPMN engine and Camunda Platform deployment
Environments requiring Python-only tooling — the Python client is less mature than Java and Go counterparts
best_when: You need high-throughput, horizontally scalable workflow orchestration with BPMN visual modeling and audit trails, especially in enterprise environments with process governance requirements.
avoid_when: Your team is code-first and finds BPMN XML an unnecessary abstraction layer over programmatic workflow definitions.

auth.notes: Camunda SaaS uses OAuth2 client credentials; self-hosted Zeebe can run without auth or with OAuth2 via Identity service; API keys available in Camunda Platform UI

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted unlimited; Camunda SaaS free tier available for development
pricing.paid_tiers: Camunda Platform 8 SaaS with starter, professional, and enterprise tiers
pricing.notes: Zeebe engine is Apache 2.0 open source; Camunda Platform 8 adds Operate, Tasklist, Optimize, and Connectors on top; cloud pricing based on process instances

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted scales horizontally; SaaS limits vary by tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC status codes are well-mapped to domain errors; job worker activation failures include process instance context; BPMN error events enable structured error propagation through workflow
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Process instance keys and job keys are stable identifiers; job activation uses optimistic locking; idempotent message correlation via message correlation keys prevents duplicate processing
known_agent_gotchas: BPMN process deployments are versioned but old versions continue running — deploying an updated workflow does not affect in-flight instances, requiring explicit migration if needed
Job worker long-polling timeout must be configured correctly; default values may cause premature timeouts in low-throughput development environments
gRPC streaming protocol requires persistent connections — load balancers and proxies must support HTTP/2 or job activation will silently fail
Message correlation requires exact key matching — any variation in message name or correlation key will cause messages to expire undelivered with no error surfaced to sender
Operate (the UI for monitoring) is a separate service not included in minimal Zeebe deployment; debugging workflow state without it requires direct API queries

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with client credentials is the recommended auth pattern for machine-to-machine; Camunda Connectors have built-in secret management; self-hosted deployments require separate Identity service configuration for full auth
